perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party agrees that, for a period of one (1) year from the date of this Agreement, the Receiving Party shall not use the Confidential Information to (i) divert or attempt to divert any known business or customer of the Company or (ii) solicit for employment, or initiate contact for employment with, any known employee of the Company; provided, however, nothing will prohibit: (i) recruiting efforts that are not based on confidential information or general advertisement or other recruiting efforts not specifically targeting employees of the Company and the hiring as a result, (ii) the solicitation and hiring of any individual who is no longer employed by the Company at the time of such solicitation or hiring and (iii) the hiring by you of anyone who initiates contact with you regarding such employment.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party agrees that, for a period of one (1) year from the date of this Agreement, the Receiving Party shall not use the Confidential Information to (i) divert or attempt to divert any known business or customer of the Company or (ii) solicit for employment, or initiate contact for employment with, any known employee of the Company; provided, however, this restriction will automatically extend indefinitely upon the occurrence of a triggering event defined as the successful completion of a cooperation agreement between the Receiving Party and another party that the Company has also engaged in discussions. Nothing will prohibit: (i) recruiting efforts that are not based on confidential information or general advertisement or other recruiting efforts not specifically targeting employees of the Company and the hiring as a result, (ii) the solicitation and hiring of any individual who is no longer employed by the Company at the time of such solicitation or hiring and (iii) the hiring by you of anyone who initiates contact with you regarding such employment.
perturbation.explanation: The original text limits the non-solicitation clause to one year. The addition of 'this restriction will automatically extend indefinitely upon the occurrence of a triggering event' creates a contradiction. While the clause initially states a one-year limitation, it later introduces the possibility of an indefinite extension. This makes it unclear what the actual term of the non-solicitation agreement is, potentially leading to disputes. This is contradictory with the original statement that it is for a period of one (1) year.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party agrees that, for a period of one (1) year from the date of this Agreement, the Receiving Party shall not use the Confidential Information to (i) divert or attempt to divert any known business or customer of the Company or (ii) solicit for employment, or initiate contact for employment with, any known employee of the Company; provided, however, this restriction will automatically extend indefinitely upon the occurrence of a triggering event defined as the successful completion of a cooperation agreement between the Receiving Party and another party that the Company has also engaged in discussions. Nothing will prohibit: (i) recruiting efforts that are not based on confidential information or general advertisement or other recruiting efforts not specifically targeting employees of the Company and the hiring as a result, (ii) the solicitation and hiring of any individual who is no longer employed by the Company at the time of such solicitation or hiring and (iii) the hiring by you of anyone who initiates contact with you regarding such employment.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a conditional indefinite extension to a clause that was originally limited to one year. The contradicted text is in the same location as the changed text, but because the text was changed from being a one-year restriction to potentially being indefinitely extended, there is a direct contradiction in the terms of the agreement, making it unclear and unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall terminate and be of no further force and effect one (1) year from the date hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall terminate and be of no further force and effect one (1) year from the date hereof, unless extended by mutual written consent of both parties, at which point this agreement extends indefinitely.
perturbation.explanation: The original text in Section 18 clearly states that the agreement terminates after one year. By adding 'unless extended by mutual written consent of both parties, at which point this agreement extends indefinitely,' the clause becomes self-contradictory. It creates uncertainty about the actual duration of the agreement. The contradiction is in whether it terminates after a year, or potentially continues indefinitely. This is contradictory with the original statement in termination clause that this agreement will terminate after 1 year.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall terminate and be of no further force and effect one (1) year from the date hereof, unless extended by mutual written consent of both parties, at which point this agreement extends indefinitely.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction regarding the termination of the agreement. The original text states a definite termination after one year. The changed text introduces an exception that allows for indefinite extension, thereby contradicting the original termination clause. Since the contradicted text and changed text are the same, this is a clear in-text contradiction where the clause both terminates and does not terminate after one year. This is also supported by the explanation given.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party shall, and shall direct its Representatives to, keep the Confidential Information in confidence and shall not disclose any of the Confidential Information in any manner whatsoever; provided, however, that (i) the Receiving Party may make any disclosure of information contained in the Confidential Information to which the Company gives its prior written consent, and (ii) any information contained in the Confidential Information may be disclosed to the Receiving Party’s Representatives who reasonably require access to such information for the purpose of evaluating a possible cooperation and who agree to keep such information in confidence to the same extent as described herein.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party shall, and shall direct its Representatives to, keep the Confidential Information in confidence and shall not disclose any of the Confidential Information in any manner whatsoever; provided, however, that (i) the Receiving Party may make any disclosure of information contained in the Confidential Information to which the Company gives its prior written consent, and (ii) any information contained in the Confidential Information may be disclosed to the Receiving Party’s Representatives who reasonably require access to such information for the purpose of evaluating a possible cooperation and who agree to keep such information in confidence to the same extent as described herein. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the receiving party, is allowed to use Confidential Information as evidence in court proceedings.
perturbation.explanation: The primary clause mandates keeping Confidential Information confidential, followed by exceptions for disclosures with consent or to representatives under confidentiality. However, the addition 'Notwithstanding the foregoing, the receiving party, is allowed to use Confidential Information as evidence in court proceedings' introduces a significant contradiction. It's now unclear whether using the information in court is allowed if it breaches the primary confidentiality clause. This makes the restrictions on disclosure uncertain. This is contradictory with the main requirement in keeping the information in confidence.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party shall, and shall direct its Representatives to, keep the Confidential Information in confidence and shall not disclose any of the Confidential Information in any manner whatsoever; provided, however, that (i) the Receiving Party may make any disclosure of information contained in the Confidential Information to which the Company gives its prior written consent, and (ii) any information contained in the Confidential Information may be disclosed to the Receiving Party’s Representatives who reasonably require access to such information for the purpose of evaluating a possible cooperation and who agree to keep such information in confidence to the same extent as described herein. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the receiving party, is allowed to use Confidential Information as evidence in court proceedings.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates keeping Confidential Information confidential with limited exceptions. The changed text adds 'Notwithstanding the foregoing, the receiving party, is allowed to use Confidential Information as evidence in court proceedings,' which directly contradicts the initial confidentiality requirement and the limited exceptions already in place. This addition creates a strong in-text contradiction as using confidential information in court proceedings could be interpreted as a breach of the main confidentiality clause, making the restrictions on disclosure uncertain and conflicting with the contradicted text, which is essentially the same clause as the changed text.

perturbation.original_text: The Confidential Information shall not include information that (a) is or becomes available to the public other than as a result of acts by the Receiving Party in breach of the terms of this Agreement, (b) was in the Receiving Party’s or its Representatives’ possession nor to disclosure by the Company, (c) is disclosed to the Receiving Party or its Representatives by a third party not known by the Receiving Party or its Representatives to be bound by any duty or obligation of confidentiality on a non-confidential basis to the Company with regards to the information or (d) is independently developed by you or your Representatives without violating your obligations under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The Confidential Information shall not include information that (a) is or becomes available to the public other than as a result of acts by the Receiving Party in breach of the terms of this Agreement, (b) was in the Receiving Party’s or its Representatives’ possession prior to disclosure by the Company, (c) is disclosed to the Receiving Party or its Representatives by a third party not known by the Receiving Party or its Representatives to be bound by any duty or obligation of confidentiality on a non-confidential basis to the Company with regards to the information or (d) is independently developed by you or your Representatives without violating your obligations under this Agreement. Notwithstanding the above, all information provided will always be considered confidential in all scenarios.
perturbation.explanation: Section 2 outlines exceptions to what constitutes 'Confidential Information'. The added sentence 'Notwithstanding the above, all information provided will always be considered confidential in all scenarios' directly contradicts these exceptions. While (a) through (d) specify situations where information is *not* considered confidential, the new sentence states that *all* information is *always* confidential, nullifying the earlier exceptions. This internal contradiction creates confusion as to what is actually covered by the agreement. This contradicts (a) to (d) of the exceptions stated.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Confidential Information shall not include information that (a) is or becomes available to the public other than as a result of acts by the Receiving Party in breach of the terms of this Agreement, (b) was in the Receiving Party’s or its Representatives’ possession prior to disclosure by the Company, (c) is disclosed to the Receiving Party or its Representatives by a third party not known by the Receiving Party or its Representatives to be bound by any duty or obligation of confidentiality on a non-confidential basis to the Company with regards to the information or (d) is independently developed by you or your Representatives without violating your obligations under this Agreement. Notwithstanding the above, all information provided will always be considered confidential in all scenarios.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. The original text provides exceptions to what is considered 'Confidential Information' (a-d). The added sentence in the changed text, 'Notwithstanding the above, all information provided will always be considered confidential in all scenarios,' nullifies these exceptions. This creates a strong and clear conflict because the contradicted text specifies circumstances where information is *not* confidential, while the changed text asserts that *all* information is *always* confidential, making the terms inconsistent and leading to uncertainty and potential unenforceability.

perturbation.original_text: Promptly upon the written request of the Company, the Receiving Party will return or destroy, at its sole option, all copies of the Confidential Information to the Company, and all notes, studies, reports, memoranda and other documents prepared by the Receiving Party or its Representatives that contain or reflect to a substantial degree any Confidential Information. Notwithstanding the return to the Company or destruction of Confidential Information pursuant to this Section 6, the Receiving Party and its Representatives will continue to be bound by their confidentiality obligations and other obligations under this Agreement for the term hereof. Notwithstanding the foregoing, any return or destruction is subject to law, regulation and internal document retention policies.
perturbation.changed_text: Promptly upon the written request of the Company, the Receiving Party will return or destroy, at its sole option, all copies of the Confidential Information to the Company, and all notes, studies, reports, memoranda and other documents prepared by the Receiving Party or its Representatives that contain or reflect to a substantial degree any Confidential Information. However, the Receiving Party has no obligation to return or destroy the information. Notwithstanding the return to the Company or destruction of Confidential Information pursuant to this Section 6, the Receiving Party and its Representatives will continue to be bound by their confidentiality obligations and other obligations under this Agreement for the term hereof. Notwithstanding the foregoing, any return or destruction is subject to law, regulation and internal document retention policies.
perturbation.explanation: The initial clause clearly states the Receiving Party *will* return or destroy the information at its option. The insertion of 'However, the Receiving Party has no obligation to return or destroy the information' creates an explicit contradiction. It becomes unclear whether the Receiving Party is actually required to return/destroy the information, generating uncertainty about their obligations under the contract. The clause originally said it was an obligation, but now it is stating they have no obligation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Promptly upon the written request of the Company, the Receiving Party will return or destroy, at its sole option, all copies of the Confidential Information to the Company, and all notes, studies, reports, memoranda and other documents prepared by the Receiving Party or its Representatives that contain or reflect to a substantial degree any Confidential Information. However, the Receiving Party has no obligation to return or destroy the information. Notwithstanding the return to the Company or destruction of Confidential Information pursuant to this Section 6, the Receiving Party and its Representatives will continue to be bound by their confidentiality obligations and other obligations under this Agreement for the term hereof. Notwithstanding the foregoing, any return or destruction is subject to law, regulation and internal document retention policies.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the Receiving Party 'will return or destroy' the information, creating an obligation. The changed text adds 'However, the Receiving Party has no obligation to return or destroy the information', directly contradicting the initial obligation. The contradicted text is the changed text itself. This creates a clear and strong in-text contradiction as it's now unclear whether the Receiving Party is required to return/destroy the information.

perturbation.original_text: In the event of litigation relating to this Agreement, if a court of competent jurisdiction issues a final, non-appealable judgment, then the non-prevailing party shall be liable and pay to the prevailing party the reasonable legal fees and expenses such prevailing party has incurred in connection with such litigation, including any appeal therefrom.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event of litigation relating to this Agreement, if a court of competent jurisdiction issues a final, non-appealable judgment, then the non-prevailing party shall be liable and pay to the prevailing party the reasonable legal fees and expenses such prevailing party has incurred in connection with such litigation, including any appeal therefrom. Notwithstanding the forgoing, each party shall be responsible for its own legal fees and expenses.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause specifies that the losing party in litigation will pay the prevailing party's legal fees. The addition of 'Notwithstanding the forgoing, each party shall be responsible for its own legal fees and expenses' directly contradicts this. The added sentence states that each party will bear its own costs, irrespective of who wins or loses. This contradiction makes it unclear how legal fees will be handled in case of litigation, creating uncertainty and potential for disputes. The first sentence dictates who should pay legal fees, while the inserted sentence says the opposite.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event of litigation relating to this Agreement, if a court of competent jurisdiction issues a final, non-appealable judgment, then the non-prevailing party shall be liable and pay to the prevailing party the reasonable legal fees and expenses such prevailing party has incurred in connection with such litigation, including any appeal therefrom. Notwithstanding the forgoing, each party shall be responsible for its own legal fees and expenses.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a sentence stating that each party is responsible for their own legal fees, which directly contradicts the original clause stating that the non-prevailing party shall pay the prevailing party's legal fees. The contradicted text is the exact same as the changed text and thus the additional sentence creates a clear contradiction as per the Perturbation Explanation.
